import random
import curses

# Set up the screen
screen = curses.initscr()
curses.curs_set(0)
screen_height, screen_width = screen.getmaxyx()
game_window = curses.newwin(screen_height, screen_width, 0, 0)
game_window.keypad(1)

# Snake initial position and length
snake = [(screen_height//2, screen_width//2)]
snake_length = 1

# Food position
food = (random.randint(1, screen_height-2), random.randint(1, screen_width-2))

# Game loop
while True:
    # Get the next move from the user
    key = game_window.getch()
    
    # Move the snake
    if key == curses.KEY_UP:
        new_head = (snake[0][0]-1, snake[0][1])
    elif key == curses.KEY_DOWN:
        new_head = (snake[0][0]+1, snake[0][1])
    elif key == curses.KEY_LEFT:
        new_head = (snake[0][0], snake[0][1]-1)
    elif key == curses.KEY_RIGHT:
        new_head = (snake[0][0], snake[0][1]+1)
    else:
        continue
    
    # Check for collisions
    if new_head in snake or new_head[0] < 1 or new_head[0] >= screen_height-1 or new_head[1] < 1 or new_head[1] >= screen_width-1:
        break
    
    # Move the snake
    snake.insert(0, new_head)
    if new_head == food:
        food = (random.randint(1, screen_height-2), random.randint(1, screen_width-2))
        snake_length += 1
    else:
        snake.pop()
    
    # Clear the screen and draw the game
    game_window.clear()
    for segment in snake:
        game_window.addch(segment[0], segment[1], '#')
    game_window.addch(food[0], food[1], '@')
    game_window.refresh()

# Clean up and exit
curses.endwin()

import random
import time
import sys

# Set up the game grid
width = 20
height = 20
grid = [[" " for x in range(width)] for y in range(height)]

# Define the initial snake position and direction
snake_x = 10
snake_y = 10
snake_length = 3
snake_direction = "right"

# Define the initial food position
food_x = random.randint(0, width-1)
food_y = random.randint(0, height-1)

# Function to update the game grid
def update_grid():
    global grid, snake_x, snake_y, snake_length, food_x, food_y
    grid = [[" " for x in range(width)] for y in range(height)]
    for i in range(snake_length):
        grid[snake_y-i][snake_x] = "*"
    grid[food_y][food_x] = "O"

# Function to move the snake
def move_snake():
    global snake_x, snake_y, snake_length, snake_direction
    if snake_direction == "right":
        snake_x += 1
    elif snake_direction == "left":
        snake_x -= 1
    elif snake_direction == "up":
        snake_y -= 1
    elif snake_direction == "down":
        snake_y += 1
    if snake_x < 0:
        snake_x = width-1
    elif snake_x >= width:
        snake_x = 0
    if snake_y < 0:
        snake_y = height-1
    elif snake_y >= height:
        snake_y = 0

# Function to check if the snake has collided with itself or the border
def check_collision():
    global snake_x, snake_y, snake_length, grid
    if grid[snake_y][snake_x] == "*" and (snake_x, snake_y) != (snake_x, snake_y-snake_length+1):
        return True
    return False

# Function to check if the snake has found food
def check_food():
    global snake_x, snake_y, food_x, food_y
    if snake_x == food_x and snake_y == food_y:
        return True
    return False

# Game loop
while True:
    update_grid()
    for row in grid:
        print(" ".join(row))
    print()
    if check_collision():
        print("Game Over!")
        sys.exit()
    if check_food():
        snake_length += 1
        food_x = random.randint(0, width-1)
        food_y = random.randint(0, height-1)
    key = input("Use arrow keys to move the snake: ")
    if key == "up":
        snake_direction = "up"
    elif key == "down":
        snake_direction = "down"
    elif key == "left":
        snake_direction = "left"
    elif key == "right":
        snake_direction = "right"
    move_snake()
    time.sleep(0.1)
    print("\033[H\033[J", end="")

import pygame
import random

# Initialize Pygame
pygame.init()

# Set up the game window
WINDOW_WIDTH = 800
WINDOW_HEIGHT = 600
game_window = pygame.display.set_mode((WINDOW_WIDTH, WINDOW_HEIGHT))
pygame.display.set_caption("Snake Game")

# Define colors
BLACK = (0, 0, 0)
WHITE = (255, 255, 255)
RED = (255, 0, 0)
GREEN = (0, 255, 0)

# Define the snake
SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE = 20
snake_blocks = []
snake_length = 3
snake_x = WINDOW_WIDTH // 2
snake_y = WINDOW_HEIGHT // 2

# Define the food
food_x = random.randint(SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, WINDOW_WIDTH - SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE)
food_y = random.randint(SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, WINDOW_HEIGHT - SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE)

# Game loop
game_over = False
clock = pygame.time.Clock()

while not game_over:
    for event in pygame.event.get():
        if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
            game_over = True
        elif event.type == pygame.KEYDOWN:
            if event.key == pygame.K_LEFT:
                dx = -SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE
                dy = 0
            elif event.key == pygame.K_RIGHT:
                dx = SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE
                dy = 0
            elif event.key == pygame.K_UP:
                dx = 0
                dy = -SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE
            elif event.key == pygame.K_DOWN:
                dx = 0
                dy = SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE

    # Move the snake
    new_x = snake_x + dx
    new_y = snake_y + dy
    snake_blocks.insert(0, (new_x, new_y))
    if len(snake_blocks) > snake_length:
        snake_blocks.pop()

    # Check for collision with the border or the snake's body
    if new_x < SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE or new_x >= WINDOW_WIDTH - SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE or \
       new_y < SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE or new_y >= WINDOW_HEIGHT - SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE or \
       (new_x, new_y) in snake_blocks[1:]:
        game_over = True

    # Check for food collision
    if new_x == food_x and new_y == food_y:
        snake_length += 1
        food_x = random.randint(SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, WINDOW_WIDTH - SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE)
        food_y = random.randint(SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, WINDOW_HEIGHT - SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE)

    # Draw the game
    game_window.fill(BLACK)
    for block in snake_blocks:
        pygame.draw.rect(game_window, WHITE, (block[0], block[1], SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE))
    pygame.draw.rect(game_window, RED, (food_x, food_y, SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE))
    pygame.display.flip()

    # Limit the frame rate
    clock.tick(10)

# Quit Pygame
pygame.quit()

import pygame
import random

# Initialize Pygame
pygame.init()

# Set up the game window
WINDOW_WIDTH = 800
WINDOW_HEIGHT = 600
game_window = pygame.display.set_mode((WINDOW_WIDTH, WINDOW_HEIGHT))
pygame.display.set_caption("Snake Game")

# Define colors
BLACK = (0, 0, 0)
WHITE = (255, 255, 255)
RED = (255, 0, 0)
GREEN = (0, 255, 0)

# Define game constants
CELL_SIZE = 20
GRID_WIDTH = WINDOW_WIDTH // CELL_SIZE
GRID_HEIGHT = WINDOW_HEIGHT // CELL_SIZE
SNAKE_SPEED = 10

# Initialize the snake
snake = [(GRID_WIDTH // 2, GRID_HEIGHT // 2)]
snake_direction = (0, 1)

# Generate the first food
food_x = random.randint(0, GRID_WIDTH - 1)
food_y = random.randint(0, GRID_HEIGHT - 1)

# Game loop
running = True
while running:
    # Handle events
    for event in pygame.event.get():
        if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
            running = False
        elif event.type == pygame.KEYDOWN:
            if event.key == pygame.K_LEFT:
                snake_direction = (-1, 0)
            elif event.key == pygame.K_RIGHT:
                snake_direction = (1, 0)
            elif event.key == pygame.K_UP:
                snake_direction = (0, -1)
            elif event.key == pygame.K_DOWN:
                snake_direction = (0, 1)

    # Move the snake
    new_head = (snake[-1][0] + snake_direction[0], snake[-1][1] + snake_direction[1])
    if new_head[0] < 0 or new_head[0] >= GRID_WIDTH or new_head[1] < 0 or new_head[1] >= GRID_HEIGHT or new_head in snake:
        # Game over
        break
    snake.append(new_head)
    if new_head != (food_x, food_y):
        snake.pop(0)
    else:
        # Generate a new food
        food_x = random.randint(0, GRID_WIDTH - 1)
        food_y = random.randint(0, GRID_HEIGHT - 1)

    # Draw the game
    game_window.fill(BLACK)
    for segment in snake:
        pygame.draw.rect(game_window, WHITE, (segment[0] * CELL_SIZE, segment[1] * CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE))
    pygame.draw.rect(game_window, RED, (food_x * CELL_SIZE, food_y * CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE, CELL_SIZE))
    pygame.display.flip()

    # Limit the frame rate
    pygame.time.Clock().tick(SNAKE_SPEED)

# Quit Pygame
pygame.quit()

import curses
import random

# Initialize the game window
screen = curses.initscr()
curses.curs_set(0)
screen_height, screen_width = screen.getmaxyx()
game_window = curses.newwin(screen_height-2, screen_width-2, 1, 1)
game_window.keypad(1)

# Set up the initial snake and food
snake = [(screen_height//2, screen_width//2)]
food = (random.randint(1, screen_height-2), random.randint(1, screen_width-2))
game_window.addch(food[0], food[1], '#')

# Game loop
score = 0
while True:
    # Get the next move
    key = game_window.getch()
    if key == curses.KEY_UP:
        new_head = (snake[0][0]-1, snake[0][1])
    elif key == curses.KEY_DOWN:
        new_head = (snake[0][0]+1, snake[0][1])
    elif key == curses.KEY_LEFT:
        new_head = (snake[0][0], snake[0][1]-1)
    elif key == curses.KEY_RIGHT:
        new_head = (snake[0][0], snake[0][1]+1)
    else:
        continue

    # Check for collisions
    if new_head in snake or new_head[0] < 1 or new_head[0] >= screen_height-1 or new_head[1] < 1 or new_head[1] >= screen_width-1:
        break

    # Move the snake
    snake.insert(0, new_head)
    if new_head == food:
        score += 1
        food = (random.randint(1, screen_height-2), random.randint(1, screen_width-2))
        game_window.addch(food[0], food[1], '#')
    else:
        game_window.delch(snake.pop()[0], snake.pop()[1])

    # Draw the snake and food
    game_window.clear()
    for segment in snake:
        game_window.addch(segment[0], segment[1], '@')
    game_window.addch(food[0], food[1], '#')
    game_window.refresh()

# Clean up and display the final score
curses.endwin()
print(f"Game over! Your score is {score}.")

import time
import random
import os
import sys
import tty
import termios

# Game dimensions
GAME_WIDTH = 30
GAME_HEIGHT = 20

# Snake initial length and starting position
SNAKE_INITIAL_LENGTH = 3
SNAKE_START_X = GAME_WIDTH // 2
SNAKE_START_Y = GAME_HEIGHT // 2

# Food spawning interval (seconds)
FOOD_SPAWN_INTERVAL = 5

# Game over message
GAME_OVER_MESSAGE = "Game Over!"

# Initialize the game state
snake = [(SNAKE_START_X, SNAKE_START_Y)]
food = None
direction = 'right'
game_running = True

def get_char():
    fd = sys.stdin.fileno()
    old_settings = termios.tcgetattr(fd)
    try:
        tty.setraw(sys.stdin.fileno())
        ch = sys.stdin.read(1)
    finally:
        termios.tcsetattr(fd, termios.TCSADRAIN, old_settings)
    return ch

def update_direction(new_direction):
    global direction
    if (direction == 'up' and new_direction != 'down') or \
       (direction == 'down' and new_direction != 'up') or \
       (direction == 'left' and new_direction != 'right') or \
       (direction == 'right' and new_direction != 'left'):
        direction = new_direction

def move_snake():
    global snake, direction
    head_x, head_y = snake[0]
    if direction == 'up':
        new_head = (head_x, head_y - 1)
    elif direction == 'down':
        new_head = (head_x, head_y + 1)
    elif direction == 'left':
        new_head = (head_x - 1, head_y)
    else:
        new_head = (head_x + 1, head_y)

    if new_head in snake or new_head[0] < 0 or new_head[0] >= GAME_WIDTH or new_head[1] < 0 or new_head[1] >= GAME_HEIGHT:
        return False

    snake.insert(0, new_head)
    if food != new_head:
        snake.pop()
    else:
        spawn_food()
    return True

def spawn_food():
    global food
    while True:
        food_x = random.randint(0, GAME_WIDTH - 1)
        food_y = random.randint(0, GAME_HEIGHT - 1)
        if (food_x, food_y) not in snake:
            food = (food_x, food_y)
            break

def draw_game():
    os.system('clear')
    for y in range(GAME_HEIGHT):
        for x in range(GAME_WIDTH):
            if (x, y) == food:
                print('F', end='')
            elif (x, y) in snake:
                print('S', end='')
            else:
                print('.', end='')
        print()

def game_loop():
    global game_running
    last_food_spawn = time.time()

    while game_running:
        draw_game()

        char = get_char()
        if char == 'w':
            update_direction('up')
        elif char == 's':
            update_direction('down')
        elif char == 'a':
            update_direction('left')
        elif char == 'd':
            update_direction('right')

        if not move_snake():
            game_running = False
            print(GAME_OVER_MESSAGE)
            break

        if time.time() - last_food_spawn >= FOOD_SPAWN_INTERVAL:
            spawn_food()
            last_food_spawn = time.time()

        time.sleep(0.1)

game_loop()

WHITE = (255, 255, 255)
RED = (255, 0, 0)
GREEN = (0, 255, 0)

# Define snake and food
snake_block_size = 20
snake_speed = 15

class Snake:
    def __init__(self, window):
        self.window = window
        self.x = window_width // 2
        self.y = window_height // 2
        self.snake_body = [(self.x, self.y)]
        self.direction = "RIGHT"
        self.length = 1

    def move(self, direction):
        if direction == "RIGHT":
            self.x += snake_block_size
        elif direction == "LEFT":
            self.x -= snake_block_size
        elif direction == "UP":
            self.y -= snake_block_size
        elif direction == "DOWN":
            self.y += snake_block_size

        self.snake_body.insert(0, (self.x, self.y))
        self.snake_body.pop()
        self.direction = direction

    def grow(self):
        self.length += 1
        self.snake_body.append((self.x, self.y))

    def draw(self):
        for segment in self.snake_body:
            pygame.draw.rect(self.window, GREEN, [segment[0], segment[1], snake_block_size, snake_block_size])

class Food:
    def __init__(self, window):
        self.window = window
        self.x = random.randint(0, (window_width - snake_block_size) // snake_block_size) * snake_block_size
        self.y = random.randint(0, (window_height - snake_block_size) // snake_block_size) * snake_block_size

    def draw(self):
        pygame.draw.rect(self.window, RED, [self.x, self.y, snake_block_size, snake_block_size])

def game_loop():
    game_over = False
    snake = Snake(window)
    food = Food(window)

    while not game_over:
        for event in pygame.event.get():
            if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
                game_over = True
            elif event.type == pygame.KEYDOWN:
                if event.key == pygame.K_LEFT and snake.direction != "RIGHT":
                    snake.direction = "LEFT"
                elif event.key == pygame.K_RIGHT and snake.direction != "LEFT":
                    snake.direction = "RIGHT"
                elif event.key == pygame.K_UP and snake.direction != "DOWN":
                    snake.direction = "UP"
                elif event.key == pygame.K_DOWN and snake.direction != "UP":
                    snake.direction = "DOWN"

pygame.init()

# Set up the game window
WINDOW_WIDTH = 800
WINDOW_HEIGHT = 600
game_window = pygame.display.set_mode((WINDOW_WIDTH, WINDOW_HEIGHT))
pygame.display.set_caption("Snake Game")

# Define colors
BLACK = (0, 0, 0)
WHITE = (255, 255, 255)
RED = (255, 0, 0)
GREEN = (0, 255, 0)

# Snake parameters
SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE = 20
SNAKE_SPEED = 10

# Food parameters
FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE = 20

# Game loop variables
game_over = False
clock = pygame.time.Clock()

# Snake class
class Snake:
    def __init__(self):
        self.length = 1
        self.positions = [(WINDOW_WIDTH//2, WINDOW_HEIGHT//2)]
        self.direction = (0, 0)

    def get_head_position(self):
        return self.positions[0]

    def turn(self, point):
        if self.length > 1 and (-point[0], -point[1]) == self.direction:
            return
        self.direction = point

    def move(self):
        cur = self.get_head_position()
        x, y = self.direction
        new_pos = (cur[0] + x * SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, cur[1] + y * SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE)
        if len(self.positions) > 2 and new_pos in self.positions[2:]:
            raise Exception("Snake has run into itself!")
        self.positions.insert(0, new_pos)
        self.positions.pop()

    def grow(self):
        self.length += 1
        self.positions.append(self.positions[-1])

    def draw(self, surface):
        for position in self.positions:
            rect = pygame.Rect((position[0], position[1]), (SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE, SNAKE_BLOCK_SIZE))
            pygame.draw.rect(surface, GREEN, rect)

# Food class
class Food:
    def __init__(self):
        self.position = (0, 0)
        self.randomize_position()

    def randomize_position(self):
        self.position = (random.randint(0, (WINDOW_WIDTH - FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE) // FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE) * FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE,
                         random.randint(0, (WINDOW_HEIGHT - FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE) // FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE) * FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE)

    def draw(self, surface):
        rect = pygame.Rect((self.position[0], self.position[1]), (FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE, FOOD_BLOCK_SIZE))
        pygame.draw.rect(surface, RED, rect)

# Game loop
snake = Snake()
food = Food()

while not game_over:
    for event in pygame.event.get():
        if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
            game_over = True
        elif event.type == pygame.KEYDOWN:
            if event.key == pygame.K_LEFT:
                snake.turn((-1, 0))
            elif event.key == pygame.K_RIGHT:
                snake.turn((1, 0))
            elif event.key == pygame.K_UP:
                snake.turn((0, -1))
            elif event.key == pygame.K_DOWN:
                snake.turn((0, 1))

    try:
        snake.move()
    except Exception as e:
        print(e)
        game_over = True

    if snake.get_head_position() == food.position:
        snake.grow()
        food.randomize_position()

    game_window.fill(BLACK)
    snake.draw(game_window)
    food.draw(game_window)
    pygame.display.flip()

    clock.tick(SNAKE_SPEED)

pygame.quit()

import time
import random

# Game dimensions
GAME_WIDTH = 50
GAME_HEIGHT = 30

# Snake constants
SNAKE_START_LENGTH = 3
SNAKE_INITIAL_SPEED = 0.2
SNAKE_SPEED_INCREASE = 0.02
SNAKE_BODY_CHAR = '#'
SNAKE_HEAD_CHAR = '@'

# Food constants
FOOD_CHAR = '$'

# Initialize the game
def initialize_game():
    game_board = [[' ' for _ in range(GAME_WIDTH)] for _ in range(GAME_HEIGHT)]
    snake = [(GAME_WIDTH//2, GAME_HEIGHT//2)]
    for i in range(1, SNAKE_START_LENGTH):
        snake.append((snake[-1][0]-1, snake[-1][1]))
    food = (random.randint(0, GAME_WIDTH-1), random.randint(0, GAME_HEIGHT-1))
    return game_board, snake, food

# Render the game board
def render_game(game_board, snake, food):
    game_board = [row[:] for row in game_board]
    for x, y in snake:
        game_board[y][x] = SNAKE_BODY_CHAR
    game_board[snake[0][1]][snake[0][0]] = SNAKE_HEAD_CHAR
    game_board[food[1]][food[0]] = FOOD_CHAR
    for row in game_board:
        print(''.join(row))

# Move the snake
def move_snake(snake, direction):
    new_head = (snake[0][0], snake[0][1])
    if direction == 'up':
        new_head = (new_head[0], new_head[1]-1)
    elif direction == 'down':
        new_head = (new_head[0], new_head[1]+1)
    elif direction == 'left':
        new_head = (new_head[0]-1, new_head[1])
    elif direction == 'right':
        new_head = (new_head[0]+1, new_head[1])
    snake.insert(0, new_head)
    snake.pop()
    return snake

# Check for collisions
def check_collision(snake, game_width, game_height):
    head = snake[0]
    if head[0] < 0 or head[0] >= game_width or head[1] < 0 or head[1] >= game_height or head in snake[1:]:
        return True
    return False

# Generate new food
def generate_food(game_board, snake):
    while True:
        food = (random.randint(0, GAME_WIDTH-1), random.randint(0, GAME_HEIGHT-1))
        if food not in snake:
            return food

# Play the game
def play_game():
    game_board, snake, food = initialize_game()
    snake_speed = SNAKE_INITIAL_SPEED
    direction = 'right'

    while True:
        render_game(game_board, snake, food)
        if check_collision(snake, GAME_WIDTH, GAME_HEIGHT):
            print("Game Over!")
            break

        direction = input("Move the snake (up/down/left/right): ")
        snake = move_snake(snake, direction)

        if snake[0] == food:
            snake.append(snake[-1])

import time
import random

class Hydra:
    def __init__(self, screen_width, screen_height):
        self.screen_width = screen_width
        self.screen_height = screen_height
        self.head_x = random.randint(0, screen_width)
        self.head_y = random.randint(0, screen_height)
        self.body_parts = [(self.head_x, self.head_y)]
        self.score = 0

    def move_head(self, dx, dy):
        self.head_x += dx
        self.head_y += dy
        self.body_parts.insert(0, (self.head_x, self.head_y))
        if self.head_x < 0 or self.head_x >= self.screen_width or self.head_y < 0 or self.head_y >= self.screen_height or (self.head_x, self.head_y) in self.body_parts[1:]:
            return False
        self.score += 1
        if len(self.body_parts) > self.score:
            self.body_parts.pop()
        return True

    def draw(self, screen):
        for x, y in self.body_parts:
            screen.set_at((x, y), (255, 0, 0))

def main():
    screen_width, screen_height = 800, 600
    hydra = Hydra(screen_width, screen_height)

    while True:
        dx, dy = 0, 0
        for event in pygame.event.get():
            if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
                return
            elif event.type == pygame.MOUSEMOTION:
                x, y = event.pos
                dx = x - hydra.head_x
                dy = y - hydra.head_y
                dx = max(-1, min(1, dx))
                dy = max(-1, min(1, dy))

        if not hydra.move_head(dx, dy):
            break

        screen.fill((0, 0, 0))
        hydra.draw(screen)
        pygame.display.flip()
        time.sleep(0.1)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    import pygame
    pygame.init()
    screen = pygame.display.set_mode((800, 600))
    main()

# Define the Hydra class
class Hydra:
    def __init__(self, x, y):
        self.x = x
        self.y = y
        self.length = 3
        self.body = [(x, y), (x-20, y), (x-40, y)]
        self.direction = "right"
        self.food_x = random.randint(50, window_width-50)
        self.food_y = random.randint(50, window_height-50)

    def move(self):
        if self.direction == "right":
            self.x += 20
        elif self.direction == "left":
            self.x -= 20
        elif self.direction == "up":
            self.y -= 20
        elif self.direction == "down":
            self.y += 20

        self.body.pop(0)
        self.body.append((self.x, self.y))

    def grow(self):
        self.length += 1
        self.body.append((self.body[-1][0], self.body[-1][1]))

    def draw(self):
        for segment in self.body:
            pygame.draw.rect(window, GREEN, (segment[0], segment[1], 20, 20))
        pygame.draw.rect(window, WHITE, (self.food_x, self.food_y, 20, 20))

        hydra.food_x = random.randint(50, window_width-50)
        hydra.food_y = random.randint(50, window_height-50)

import random

window_height = 600

pygame.display.set_caption("Hydra")

WHITE = (255, 255, 255)
BLUE = (0, 0, 255)

hydra_size = 20
hydra_x = window_width // 2
hydra_y = window_height // 2
hydra_parts = [(hydra_x, hydra_y)]

dx = 0
dy = 0

food_x = random.randint(hydra_size, window_width - hydra_size)
food_y = random.randint(hydra_size, window_height - hydra_size)

# Game loop
running = True
while running:
    # Handle events
    for event in pygame.event.get():
        if event.type == pygame.QUIT:
            running = False
        elif event.type == pygame.KEYDOWN:
            if event.key == pygame.K_LEFT:
                dx = -hydra_size
                dy = 0
            elif event.key == pygame.K_RIGHT:
                dx = hydra_size
                dy = 0
            elif event.key == pygame.K_UP:
                dx = 0
                dy = -hydra_size
            elif event.key == pygame.K_DOWN:
                dx = 0
                dy = hydra_size

    # Move the Hydra
    new_head_x = hydra_parts[-1][0] + dx
    new_head_y = hydra_parts[-1][1] + dy
    hydra_parts.append((new_head_x, new_head_y))
    hydra_parts.pop(0)

    # Check for collision with the "food"
    if abs(new_head_x - food_x) < hydra_size and abs(new_head_y - food_y) < hydra_size:
        food_x = random.randint(hydra_size, window_width - hydra_size)
        food_y = random.randint(hydra_size, window_height - hydra_size)
        hydra_parts.insert(0, hydra_parts[0])

    # Check for collision with the game window boundaries
    if new_head_x < hydra_size or new_head_x > window_width - hydra_size or \
       new_head_y < hydra_size or new_head_y > window_height - hydra_size:
        running = False

    # Clear the game window
    window.fill(BLACK)

    # Draw the Hydra
    for part in hydra_parts:
        pygame.draw.rect(window, BLUE, (part[0], part[1], hydra_size, hydra_size))

    # Draw the "food"
    pygame.draw.rect(window, WHITE, (food_x, food_y, hydra_size, hydra_size))

    # Update the game window
    pygame.display.flip()

# Quit Pygame
